title: Flash loan attack via callback + skim allows stealing pool funds
reason: `swap()` and `skim()` share the same `lock` modifier, so `skim()` cannot be reentered from `NimbusCall`; the proposed exploit path is blocked.

title: DOMAIN_SEPARATOR not updated on chain fork leading to signature reuse
reason: The code matches an older fixed-domain permit pattern, but the risk is fork-contingent and too speculative for a reportable issue in this audit.

title: Swap callback allows arbitrary external calls enabling potential attack vectors
reason: This is the standard flash-swap callback pattern; no concrete exploit survives the pair's reentrancy lock.

title: Hardcoded magic number for infinite approval
reason: Readability issue only; not a security vulnerability.

title: No check for zero-address in setFeeTo
reason: `setFeeTo` is not implemented in this contract, and a zero `feeTo` is the normal way to disable fees.

title: Missing sync before K check
reason: The invariant check uses live token balances read immediately before validation, so a prior `sync()` is unnecessary.

title: Anyone can steal tokens accidentally sent to the pair via skim
reason: `skim()` is an intentional Uniswap-style dust recovery mechanism that only transfers balances above recorded reserves; this is by design rather than a distinct vulnerability.
